comments.author: chatgpt-codex-connector
comments.body:
Codex usage limits have been reached for code reviews. Please check with the admins of this repo to increase the limits by adding credits.
Credits must be used to enable repository wide code reviews.

reviews.author: linyueqian
reviews.body:
Approving. The core fix is correct: `tts_postprocess` has emitted nested `codes.audio` since #4527, while `tts_preprocess` still only read the legacy top-level `audio`, so decode feedback was silently dropped. This PR closes that gap and adds regression tests for both payload shapes.

One non-blocking comment: the new short-transcript containment fallback runs for all callers of `_assert_transcript_matches`, but the docstring ("When `escalation_model` is `None` the original strict behaviour is preserved, so other tests are unaffected") and the module comment above it still describe the old strict contract. Since this helper backs the whole speech test suite, please update those two doc spots to mention the containment fallback.

https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-omni/blob/c4f493ebd7deceedef57a14336bfd2c0af04ba36/tests/helpers/assertions.py#L644-L657

Minor, feel free to ignore: `tts_preprocess` checks legacy `audio` before `codes.audio` (opposite of the PR description's nested-first framing) — harmless today since producers emit only one shape, but worth confirming the legacy fallback still has a live producer at all, given #4527 intentionally moved away from the top-level `audio` key.

https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-omni/blob/c4f493ebd7deceedef57a14336bfd2c0af04ba36/vllm_omni/model_executor/models/voxtral_tts/voxtral_tts.py#L211-L217
